content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
· 428 ,J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Noveed Safipour 
President 
Peninsula Young Democrats 
1516 S. Claremont Street, Apt. F 
San Mateo, CA 94402 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-16-193 
Dear Mr. Safipour: 
October 14, 2016 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the campaign provisions of the 
Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 This letter is based on the facts presented. The Fair Political 
Practices Commission (the "Commission") does not act as a finder of fact when it renders advice. 
(In re Oglesby ( 1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.) 
QUESTION 
Does the Act authorize the Peninsula Young Democrats (''PYO"), a semi-independent 
political club affiliated with the Peninsula Democratic Coalition ("PDC") political club, to make 
expenditures that are not subject to disclosure, given that the PDC has an active general purpose 
committee, the PYO receives all its funding from the PDC, and the PYO shares a bank account and 
website with the PDC? 
CONCLUSION 
No. The Act requires the PDC to disclose any expenditure made by the PYO on its 
campaign reports because the PYO is a component of the PDC based on the facts presented. 
FACTS 
The Peninsula Democratic Coalition ("PDC") is a long-standing political club with members 
throughout Silicon Valley. The PDC is chartered by both the Santa Clara and San Mateo County 
Democratic Central Committees, and is affiliated with the California Democratic Council, a 
statewide coalition of grass roots clubs. The PDC has an active general purpose committee. 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


File No. A-16-193 
Page No. 2 
The Peninsula Young Democrats ("PYD") is a semi-independent political club affiliated 
with the PDC. Although the PYO exercises substantial discretion over its day-to-day activities, it 
does not have its own governing board, receives all its funding from the PDC, and shares a bank 
account and website with the PDC. Each year the PDC allocates funding to the PYO to support the 
PYD's activities. You serve as President of the PYO and as a member of the Board of Directors of 
the PDC, but you are the only overlapping member of both clubs' respective leaderships. 
In past election years, the PYO has not made expenditures or engaged in any other political 
activity that would require registration as a committee, disclosure, or reporting under the Act. The 
PYO is now considering making expenditures for the first time, and has inquired about whether it 
may make expenditures that are not subject to disclosure under the Act. 
ANALYSIS 
The Act's campaign disclosure provisions require committees to file a statement of 
organization and periodic reports disclosing contributions received and expenditures made. (Section 
84100 et seq.) Under Section 82013, a "committee" is any person or organization that receives 
contributions totaling $2,000 or more in a calendar year for political purposes, makes independent 
expenditures totaling $1,000 or more in a calendar year, or makes contributions of $10,000 or more 
in a calendar year. 
PDC has an active general purpose committee (FPPC # 1288904) that is registered with the 
Secretary of State and files periodic disclosure reports. Although the PYO exercises substantial 
discretion over its day-to-day activities, it does not have its own governing board, receives all its 
funding from the PDC, and shares a bank account and website with the PDC. Therefore, based on 
the facts presented and as currently structured, the PYO is not independent but rather a component 
of the PDC for purposes of the Act. 
Accordingly, we conclude that any expenditure by the PYO must be disclosed on the 
campaign reports of the PDC's general purpose committee. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
MFC:jgl 
Sincerely, 
By: 
Matthew F. Christy 
Counsel, Legal Division

website with the PDC?
